title: Slow-cooker honey and mustard gammon
subtitle: Gammon joint cooked in stock, then glazed
excerpt: A gammon joint slow-cooked in stock for six hours until fully tender, then slathered with honey and Dijon mustard and crisped under the grill. The slow cooker does the poaching; the grill does the glaze. Serve warm or cold.
sourceNotes: Glazed gammon is a British roasting table staple, particularly at Christmas. Traditionally the joint is first simmered in water or stock and then roasted with a glaze. The slow cooker handles the first stage without any attention, producing a more consistently tender result than a hob simmer. The honey and Dijon glaze is the most widely used British version.

recipe.freezeNotes: Slice and freeze unglazed. Glaze from fresh if serving warm.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cook and glaze the day before. Serve cold, or reheat covered in foil at 160°C for 20 minutes.

content.text: Score the fat before applying the glaze. A diamond pattern cut through the fat layer allows the glaze to penetrate and gives the finished dish the recognisable cross-hatched appearance. Cut through the fat only, not into the meat below.

Place the {{gammon}} in the slow cooker. Add the {{bay-leaves}} and {{black-pepper}}. Pour over the {{stock-chicken}} until the joint is mostly covered — top up with water if needed. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the meat is tender throughout.

Heat the oven grill to its highest setting. Remove the gammon and allow to cool slightly — 10 minutes. Peel away the outer skin, leaving the fat layer. Score the fat in a diamond pattern. Mix the {{honey}}, {{dijon-mustard}}, and {{light-brown-sugar}} together and spread thickly over the scored fat. Place on a foil-lined roasting tray and grill for 6 to 10 minutes, until caramelised and glossy. Rest for 15 minutes before slicing.

items.symptom: Glaze burning before it caramelises evenly
items.cause: Grill too close to the element or heat uneven
items.fix: Move the gammon 5 to 8 cm further from the grill element. The glaze should bubble and caramelise slowly rather than char

items.cause: The cooking stock absorbed the salt from the cured gammon
items.fix: Discard the cooking stock (it is very salty and not useful for other recipes). For a less salty result, soak the raw gammon in cold water for 2 hours before cooking

items.symptom: Fat layer peeling away from the joint
items.cause: Overcooked or the skin was removed too forcefully
items.fix: Peel the skin slowly and carefully. The fat layer should remain attached to the meat. If it separates, lay it back in place before scoring and glazing

content.text: Glazed gammon is a British table centrepiece for Christmas, Easter, and Sunday lunch. The slow cooker version makes the most technically difficult part, the long poach, completely hands-off. Serve warm with parsley sauce, roast potatoes, and green vegetables, or cold with English mustard, chutney, and crusty bread.
